prompt: Kuukikan (空気感) style hyper-realistic film photograph. South London suburban train station platform, 8:00 AM morning. A Black British woman in her early 40s (Naomi — short natural hair, smart business attire, holding a smartphone) stands near the edge of the platform, small in the frame. The platform is mostly empty with a few distant commuters. Morning diffused light filters through gaps in the platform canopy, casting soft patterns on the concrete. Railway tracks stretch into the distance, catching warm morning light reflections on the metal rails. Color grading: clean neutral tones. The concrete grey of the platform contrasts with the morning light. Railway metal reflects warm golden tones. Film-like desaturation throughout. Composition: 16:9 wide frame. Naomi is positioned in the right quarter of the frame, small. The left three-quarters are the expansive platform space, tracks, and the vanishing point of the railway — generous negative space. Platform pillars and canopy structure create natural framing. Camera: 35mm lens, f/4.0, ISO 400. Fine film grain. The tracks have beautiful old-lens bokeh as they recede. Peripheral vignette and softness. The mood is a fresh London morning — the quiet anticipation of a commute, but today something begins. Clean, still, hopeful.
negative_prompt: dramatic lighting, lens flare, HDR, oversaturated, CGI, plastic skin, studio lighting, underground tube station, crowded platform, rain, dark, text overlays, watermarks, logos, AI smoothing, digital painting, anime, cartoon, illustration
